policies.specialty_requirement: Orthopedic Surgery, Neurosurgery, or Neurology referral
clinical_prerequisites: Lower back pain radiating down the leg or signs of radiculopathy
Trial of conservative physical therapy for at least 6 weeks
Trial of conservative drug therapy (NSAIDs or analgesics) for at least 4 weeks
Diagnosis of moderate-to-severe Rheumatoid Arthritis or Plaque Psoriasis
Trial and failure of at least 2 conventional DMARDs (e.g. methotrexate, sulfasalazine) for at least 3 months each
Diagnosis of advanced osteoarthritis (ICD-10 M17.11)
Radiology report confirming Kellgren-Lawrence Grade 3 or 4 joint space narrowing
Standardized joint pain assessment score (e.g. WOMAC or KOOS) confirming severe functional limitation
Trial of conservative non-surgical management for at least 12 weeks
BMI greater than 40, or BMI greater than 35 with at least one severe obesity-related comorbidity (e.g., Type 2 Diabetes, Severe Sleep Apnea)
Documented participation in a medically supervised weight loss program for at least 6 consecutive months
Clearance from a licensed psychologist or psychiatrist evaluating readiness for surgery
Epworth Sleepiness Scale (ESS) score of 10 or greater
Witnessed apneas, gasping, or choking during sleep reported by a sleep partner
Documented symptoms of excessive daytime sleepiness not explained by other conditions
Diagnosis of Type 1 or Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
Patient requires administration of insulin at least 3 times daily or uses an insulin pump
Patient or caregiver is sufficiently trained to use the CGM device based on provider assessment
Diagnosis of chronic migraine defined as headaches on at least 15 days per month, of which at least 8 days have migraine features, for more than 3 months
Trial and failure, or intolerance, to at least 2 different classes of oral migraine preventive medications (e.g., beta-blockers, anticonvulsants, tricyclic antidepressants) for at least 8 weeks each
